rulings.Case Title: IRENE BOLLINGER VS. CALAVERAS ASBESTOS LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Clark Equipment Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 5, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Clark Equipment Company's motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed October 29, 2015. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA PONCHIONE VS. CONSOLIDATED INSULATION, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 5, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Curtis Wiggins Enterprises, Inc. dba One Stop Auto Parts' special demurrer to plaintiffs' third (negligence I - wrongful death) and fourth (products liability - wrongful death) causes of action in their First Amended Complaint is overruled. Although plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint was filed beyond the asbestos wrongful death limitations period enunciated in C.C.P. § 340.2(c), the third and fourth causes of action are not time barred because they relate back to the timely filing of plaintiffs' initial complaint. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
